scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live "api-settings" OpenAPI spec (fetched directly from the embedded page-state JSON on https://docs.split.io/reference/get-workspaces, which carries the complete 45-path spec on every reference page — server base https://api.split.io/internal/api/v2, matching this bundle's own path prefix): 7 read streams (the 4 wave2 streams retained at parity, plus groups, traffic_types, users) and 6 write actions (feature-flag kill/restore/archive/unarchive, segment key add/remove). Excluded: change requests, attributes, large segments, flag sets, rule-based segments, restrictions, rollout statuses, identities, API keys, and account-structure/CRUD-beyond-the-listed-actions — see per-endpoint reasoning below. Flag sets and large segments are a newer FME-tier surface documented on pages that did not carry embedded OpenAPI page-state during this pass's fetch (https://docs.split.io/reference/list-flag-sets.md returned a rendering without the same embedded schema); their existence is noted but not enumerated as individual excluded endpoints since no authoritative path list could be confirmed from this pass's research.

excluded.reason: creates a new project (workspace); account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation this reverse-ETL connector targets
renames/reconfigures an existing workspace; account-structure administration, not a data-sync mutation
permanently deletes a workspace and everything defined inside it (environments, flags, segments); account-structure destruction, far outside reverse-ETL write scope
creates a new FME environment; account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation
renames/reconfigures an existing environment; account-structure administration, not a data-sync mutation
permanently deletes an environment and every feature-flag/segment definition scoped to it

CreateFeatureFlagRequest's real body ('feature_flag' form field carrying a JSON-encoded string per the API's form-encoded convention) does not fit the engine's flat json/form write-body construction; feature-flag account provisioning is also a rarer/riskier reverse-ETL action than the kill/restore/archive lifecycle mutations this pass covers
single-feature-flag detail; identical record shape to the splits list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

updates a feature flag's description/tags/rollout status; narrower and lower-value than the kill/restore/archive/unarchive lifecycle actions already covered, and its body shape overlaps update_feature_flag_description without a clear single flat record shape
permanently deletes a feature flag account-wide; every calling SDK falls back to 'control' irrecoverably, a materially higher-risk mutation than archive (which is reversible via unarchive)
narrow single-field update; the archive/unarchive/kill/restore lifecycle actions already cover this pass's practical feature-flag write surface

lists feature-flag DEFINITIONS (targeting rules/treatments) scoped to one environment, a materially different and much larger record shape (rules/buckets/matchers) than the splits list stream's summary object; scoping this pass to the flag catalog, not per-environment targeting-rule detail
creates a full feature-flag definition (targeting rules, treatments, default rule) for an environment; deeply nested body shape, no corresponding read stream declared in this pass
single feature-flag-definition-in-environment detail; no corresponding list-definitions-in-environment stream is covered in this pass
full replace of a feature-flag definition (targeting rules, treatments, default rule); deeply nested body shape not expressible as a flat write record
partial update of a feature-flag definition; same deeply-nested-body limitation as the full-update endpoint

unconfigures a feature-flag definition from an environment (every calling SDK in that environment falls back to 'control'); a materially higher-risk mutation than kill (which preserves the definition and is reversible via restore)
documented POST alternate for the identical kill operation ('only use this API if PUT does not work'); the PUT variant is already covered by kill_feature_flag_in_environment

resets the bucketing seed, rehashing and reassigning every end-user to new targeting buckets; a rare, high-blast-radius operational action distinct from ordinary kill/restore/archive lifecycle management
CreateSegmentRequest's real body ('segment' form field carrying a JSON-encoded string per the API's form-encoded convention) does not fit the engine's flat json/form write-body construction
permanently deletes a segment account-wide, automatically unconfiguring it from every environment; a materially higher-risk mutation than the segment-key add/remove actions already covered
lists segments scoped to one environment; the workspace-scoped segments list stream already covers the segment catalog for this pass
activates (enables) a segment in an environment before its keys can be managed there; a one-time provisioning step distinct from the ongoing key add/remove mutations already covered
deactivates a segment in an environment, removing it from targeting rules for every feature flag using it there; higher-risk than the additive/subtractive key mutations already covered

per-segment member-key listing requires fan-out over every (environment, segment) pair discovered by the segments stream; no parent-id source stream pairing is declared in this pass, and the resulting record volume (individual end-user keys) is a different granularity than this pass's catalog-level streams
CSV file upload; the engine's write dialect has no multipart/binary body construction
creates a new user group; account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation
single-group detail; identical record shape to the groups list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
renames/reconfigures an existing group; account-structure administration, not a data-sync mutation
permanently deletes a user group, affecting every member's access; account-structure destruction, out of scope
creates a new traffic type; account-structure provisioning, not a data-sync mutation
permanently deletes a traffic type, invalidating every feature flag/segment scoped to it; account-structure destruction, out of scope
invites a new user to the account; account/identity administration, not a data-sync mutation
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates display name/email/2FA/active-deactive status; account/identity administration, not a data-sync mutation
updates a user's group memberships; account/identity administration, not a data-sync mutation
permanently deletes a pending (not-yet-accepted) user invite; account/identity administration, out of scope
mints a new Admin/SDK API key with full account access; a credential-provisioning action, not a data-sync mutation, and a materially different risk class than any other write in this bundle
permanently revokes an API key, potentially breaking every live integration still using it; credential lifecycle management, out of scope
approval-workflow surface (change requests for flag/segment definitions); a distinct governance feature from this bundle's catalog-read/lifecycle-write scope
submits a new change request; approval-workflow surface, out of scope for this pass
approval-workflow surface, out of scope for this pass
approves/rejects/withdraws a change request; approval-workflow surface, out of scope for this pass
attribute-schema surface for a traffic type; a distinct, lower-priority catalog than this pass's flag/segment/group/user objects
attribute-schema surface, out of scope for this pass
bulk attribute upload via JSON; attribute-schema surface, out of scope for this pass
attribute-schema surface, out of scope for this pass
deletes an attribute schema and its stored data for every identity in the traffic type; attribute-schema surface, out of scope for this pass
account-tier feature-restriction configuration; not syncable business data
changes account-tier feature restrictions; org-administration action, out of scope
lookup list of rollout-status labels available for a workspace; reference/lookup data, not a primary syncable object
associates tags with a feature flag or segment; a narrower metadata-only mutation than this pass's flag-lifecycle and segment-key write actions
bulk-saves per-user Split identities/attribute values; a materially different, much-higher-volume record granularity (individual end-user attribute state) than any stream this bundle covers
same identity-attribute-state surface as the bulk-save endpoint above, out of scope
same identity-attribute-state surface, out of scope
same identity-attribute-state surface, out of scope
